title: The School Bus
summary: A student describes his daily bus ride to school — the familiar faces he sees, the sounds around him, and how the journey makes him feel each morning.
body: Every morning, I walk to the bus stop at the end of my street. The bus comes at seven forty-five. I always stand near the shelter so I can stay dry when it rains. When the bus arrives, I get on and find a seat by the window. I like the window seat because I can watch the town go by. The driver is a friendly man who always says good morning to everyone. The bus is usually quite busy. There are students from my school and some adults going to work. An old man always sits in the front row with a newspaper. Two girls from my class always sit together and chat quietly. I usually listen to music on my phone. The ride is about twenty minutes long. When we get close to school, I put my phone away and get ready. Sometimes I feel a little nervous about the day. But when I see my friends at the school gate, I always feel better. The bus ride is my favourite part of the morning.

annotations.exampleEn: We waited at the bus stop for fifteen minutes.

annotations.meaningVi: mái che, lán che (ở trạm xe buýt)
annotations.exampleEn: We stood under the shelter to stay out of the rain.
On the plane, she always chooses a window seat.
I love watching the countryside go by from the train window.
The old lady always sat in the front row of the cinema.
We had a nice chat over coffee.
She read her book quietly in the corner.
He felt nervous before his exam.
Her mum always waited for her at the school gate.
When we got close to the town, I could see the church tower.

grammarNotes.title: Frequency Adverbs: always, usually, sometimes
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Trạng từ tần suất cho biết việc gì xảy ra thường xuyên ra sao. 'Always' (luôn luôn) > 'usually' (thường thường) > 'sometimes' (đôi khi). Chúng thường đứng trước động từ chính hoặc sau động từ 'to be'. Ví dụ: 'I always stand near the shelter.' / 'The bus is usually quite busy.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Frequency adverbs tell us how often something happens: 'always' (every time), 'usually' (most times), 'sometimes' (some times). They go before the main verb but after 'to be'. Examples: 'I always stand near the shelter.' / 'The bus is usually quite busy.'

grammarNotes.title: Present Simple for Routines
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì hiện tại đơn được dùng để nói về thói quen và thói quen hàng ngày. Khi chủ ngữ là ngôi thứ ba số ít (he, she, it), thêm '-s' vào động từ: 'The driver always says good morning.' / 'An old man always sits in the front row.' Chú ý không thêm 's' với 'I' hoặc 'you'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: We use the present simple tense to describe routines and habits. With third-person singular subjects (he, she, it), add '-s' to the verb: 'The driver says good morning.' / 'An old man sits in the front row.' With 'I' and 'you', do not add '-s'.

translationVi: Mỗi sáng, tôi đi bộ đến trạm xe buýt ở cuối đường. Xe buýt đến lúc bảy giờ bốn mươi lăm. Tôi luôn đứng gần mái che để không bị ướt khi trời mưa. Khi xe đến, tôi lên xe và tìm chỗ ngồi cạnh cửa sổ. Tôi thích chỗ ngồi cạnh cửa sổ vì tôi có thể nhìn phố phường trôi qua. Tài xế là một người đàn ông thân thiện, luôn chào buổi sáng với mọi người. Xe buýt thường khá đông. Có nhiều học sinh từ trường tôi và một số người lớn đi làm. Một ông cụ luôn ngồi ở hàng ghế đầu tiên với một tờ báo. Hai cô bé từ lớp tôi luôn ngồi cạnh nhau và trò chuyện nhỏ nhẹ. Tôi thường nghe nhạc trên điện thoại. Chặng đường đi khoảng hai mươi phút. Khi sắp đến trường, tôi cất điện thoại đi và chuẩn bị. Đôi khi tôi cảm thấy hơi hồi hộp về ngày hôm đó. Nhưng khi tôi thấy bạn bè ở cổng trường, tôi luôn cảm thấy vui hơn. Chuyến xe buýt là phần yêu thích nhất của tôi vào buổi sáng.

questions.prompt: What time does the bus arrive?
questions.explanation: The story says 'The bus comes at seven forty-five.'
questions.hint: Look for the specific time mentioned in the story.

questions.prompt: Why does the student stand near the shelter?
options: To stay dry when it rains
questions.explanation: The story says 'I always stand near the shelter so I can stay dry when it rains.'
questions.hint: What happens at the bus stop when it rains?

questions.prompt: Why does the student prefer a window seat?
options: He can watch the town go by
He can see his friends outside
It is closer to the door
questions.explanation: The story says 'I like the window seat because I can watch the town go by.'
questions.hint: What does the student enjoy seeing through the window?

questions.prompt: What does the bus driver do every morning?
options: He says good morning to everyone
He tells people where to sit
questions.explanation: The story says 'The driver is a friendly man who always says good morning to everyone.'
questions.hint: How does the driver greet passengers?

questions.prompt: Who sits in the front row?
options: An old woman with a book
An old man with a newspaper
questions.explanation: The story says 'An old man always sits in the front row with a newspaper.'
questions.hint: Who is the regular front-row passenger?

questions.prompt: What do the two girls from his class do on the bus?
questions.explanation: The story says 'Two girls from my class always sit together and chat quietly.'
questions.hint: What do the two classmates do during the ride?

questions.prompt: What does the student do during the bus ride?
options: Listen to music on his phone
questions.explanation: The story says 'I usually listen to music on my phone.'
questions.hint: How does the student pass the time on the bus?

questions.prompt: How long is the bus ride?
questions.explanation: The story says 'The ride is about twenty minutes long.'
questions.hint: How many minutes is the journey?

questions.prompt: How does the student sometimes feel before arriving at school?
questions.explanation: The story says 'Sometimes I feel a little nervous about the day.'
questions.hint: What emotion does the student occasionally feel near school?

questions.prompt: What makes the student feel better when he arrives?
options: Seeing his friends at the school gate
questions.explanation: The story says 'when I see my friends at the school gate, I always feel better.'
questions.hint: Who or what cheers the student up at the end of the journey?

questions.prompt: Which part of the morning does the student like most?
questions.explanation: The story says 'The bus ride is my favourite part of the morning.'
questions.hint: What does the student call his 'favourite' part?

cloze.template: Every morning, I walk to the bus stop at the end of my street. The bus comes at seven forty-five. I always stand near the {{b1}} so I can stay dry when it rains. When the bus arrives, I get on and find a seat by the {{b2}}. I like the window seat because I can watch the town go by. The driver is a {{b3}} man who always says good morning to everyone. The bus is usually quite {{b4}}. There are students from my school and some adults going to work. An old man always sits in the front row with a {{b5}}. Two girls from my class always sit together and {{b6}} quietly. I usually listen to {{b7}} on my phone. The ride is about twenty minutes long. When we get close to school, I put my phone away and get ready. Sometimes I feel a little {{b8}} about the day. But when I see my friends at the school {{b9}}, I always feel better. The bus ride is my {{b10}} part of the morning.

blanks.explanation: A bus stop 'shelter' is the covered area that protects people from rain.
A 'window seat' is a seat next to the window — great for watching outside.
The driver is described as 'friendly' because he greets everyone warmly.
'Quite busy' means there are a lot of people on the bus.
The old man reads a 'newspaper' — a printed daily news publication.
'Chat' means to have a friendly, informal conversation.
The story says the student 'listens to music on his phone' during the ride.
'Nervous' means slightly anxious or worried — often felt before something new.
The 'school gate' is the entrance to the school — a familiar meeting point.
'Favourite' means the thing you like the most — here the bus ride is the best part of his morning.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The student says the bus ride is his favourite part of the morning, even though he sometimes feels nervous about the day. Why might a journey — not the destination — be the best part of someone's day? Have you ever felt this way?
